# Pull Request Summary Checklist (Required)

This template is required for all pull requests in this course. Complete all sections before requesting instructor review.  
PRs with missing or incomplete sections may not be reviewed.

To check a box, replace [ ] with [x].

---

## Review Cycle (Select One)
- [ ] Review Cycle 1
- [ ] Review Cycle 2
- [ ] Review Cycle 3
- [ ] Review Cycle 4

---

## Branch Strategy (Required)

### During Development
Each issue must be worked on in its own dedicated branch. Do not combine work for multiple issues in a single branch.

### At Submission
Before submitting this PR, merge all your feature branches into a single submission branch. This PR should be from your submission branch into `main`.

**Submission branch name:** `submission-cycle-X` (replace X with your cycle number, e.g., `submission-cycle-1`)

**Feature branches merged into this submission:**

| Branch Name | Related Issue | Brief Description |
|-------------|---------------|-------------------|
|             | #             |                   |
|             | #             |                   |
|             | #             |                   |
|             | #             |                   |
|             | #             |                   |
|             | #             |                   |

Confirmation:
- [ ] Each issue was developed on its own separate branch (one branch per issue)
- [ ] Each feature branch is named with its issue number (e.g., `issue-12-authentication`)
- [ ] All feature branches were merged into a single submission branch before creating this PR
- [ ] Merge conflicts (if any) were resolved during consolidation

---

## Summary of Work Completed (2–4 sentences)

Describe what you completed and how it advances the milestone for the selected review cycle.

**Summary:**
<!-- Replace with your summary -->

---

## Acceptance Criteria Status

For each linked issue:
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are fully met, OR
- [ ] Any unmet criteria are clearly explained below with a plan and target cycle

**Notes (if applicable):**
<!-- Explain partial completion, deferred items, or follow-on issues -->

---

## GitHub Workflow and Branch Discipline (Required)

### Development Phase
- [ ] One branch per issue — no mixing of work across issues in a single branch
- [ ] Branch names reference issue numbers (e.g., `issue-12-authentication`)
- [ ] All commits related to an issue are on its dedicated branch
- [ ] No work was done directly on the `main` branch

### Submission Phase
- [ ] All feature branches were merged into one submission branch
- [ ] This PR is from the submission branch into `main`
- [ ] This PR links all issues it addresses

---

## Incremental Progress (Anti One-Shot Requirement)

- [ ] Commit history shows incremental progress over time (not a last-minute dump)
- [ ] Work evolved through multiple commits per issue branch

---

## Known Limitations / Deferred Work / Next Steps

- [ ] Any known limitations or deferred work are documented below
- [ ] Deferred work is captured as new or updated GitHub Issue(s)

**Notes:**
<!-- List limitations and link follow-up issues if created -->

---

## PR Timing and Merge Acknowledgement (Required)

- [ ] PR submitted by the PR due day
- [ ] I understand instructor approval is required before merge
- [ ] I will merge this PR by the merge due day after approval
- [ ] I understand work is considered complete only after merge into `main`

---
